content: Tagged current HEAD as v0.4.7 so the release includes the transport regression coverage and the aligned runtime version metadata.
Keep the canonical release checklist in RELEASE_CHECKLIST.md and point AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md to it, so the workflow stays centralized instead of drifting across duplicate copies.
Kept the optional PDF exports public by re-exporting them from a module alias in __init__.py, preserving the API while removing Ruff F401 warnings.
Put the post-v0.4.7 release checklist and lint cleanup under Unreleased > Changed because they are the only notable changes after the tag and they do not alter NFSe runtime behavior.
Move the Prestador.inscricao_municipal note from Unreleased into 0.4.7 because the implementation landed in e46a83b1 on 2026-06-04, before the v0.4.7 tag commit c4530ff.

Encoded cIndOp from the official gov.br ANEXO_C workbook instead of guessing the RTC list; current workbook yields 26 source codes and marks 080101 as Via-only, so the repo table excludes it for this library.

The official ANEXO_C workbook groups cIndOp by category/variant rows (for example 0301 -> 030101..030104) and does not contain literal vAggr/vDet fields; the grouped breakdown is the annex row hierarchy, not XML elements.
cIndOp now uses a Literal allowlist plus a pre-validator that raises NFSeValidationError with redacted messages, so invalid operation codes fail strictly without echoing the raw input.

Implemented query_nfse_by_dps(id_dps) as a two-step flow: GET /dps/{id} to recover chaveAcesso, then the existing NFSe-by-key query to return NFSeQueryResult. This keeps the public API typed and avoids duplicating the NFSe parsing path.

The official Manual dos Contribuintes exposes DPS recovery as GET /dps/{id} and existence check as HEAD /dps/{id}; GET returns the DPS-related access key, and the client can then reuse the existing key-based NFSe query path to fetch full invoice details.

Keep emitting regApIBSCBSSN and opSimpNac=4 per epic locked decisions; fix the vendored XSD fixture instead because local TCRegTrib/TSOpSimpNac is stale relative to the IBSCBS implementation path under test.

The vendored DPS_v1.01 fixture only patched TSSerieDPS before this bead; TCRegTrib still rejected regApIBSCBSSN and TSOpSimpNac still capped the enum at 3, so local XSD validation lagged behind the IBSCBS Simples model/builder surface.

A generic string replacement against XSD enumeration blocks is too broad here; TSOpSimpNac must be patched via a schema-specific anchor or it will mutate unrelated 1/2/3 enums without affecting the Simples Nacional definition used by DPS validation.

When patching vendored NFSe XSD fixtures, anchor replacements to named schema blocks like TSOpSimpNac or TCRegTrib instead of generic enumeration snippets; broad string replacements can silently mutate unrelated enums and make the local validator untrustworthy.

Vendored 1.01 DPS XSD still exposes TSRTCFinNFSe={0} and omits tpNFSeCredito/tpNFSeDebito from TCRTCInfoIBSCBS, while library models/builder already support finNFSe 1/2 adjustment paths. Local XSD validation therefore rejects valid builder output until fixture generator and vendored schema are patched together.

Address UF is not present in TCEnderNac on IBSCBS response paths, so EnderecoIBSCBS.uf must be optional and response_parsers.parse_ibscbs must stop synthesizing UF='00'. Keep codigo_municipio+CEP as the address identity carried by the XML.

IBSCBS response addresses use end/endNac with cMun+CEP but no UF, so parser/model must treat UF as optional transport data rather than mandatory schema data or the whole subtree gets dropped during model validation.

Treat malformed 200 query payloads as API errors, not partial success. _query_nfse_with_client now should require dict JSON plus chaveAcesso, dhEmi, and at least one nNFSe source (XML or JSON) before constructing NFSeQueryResult.

Query endpoints should never index JSON payloads with mandatory-key semantics after a 200. Validate dict shape and required business fields first, then raise NFSeAPIError for malformed success payloads so callers get one exception contract.

For IBSCBS xs:choice groups, model the choice explicitly with Optional submodels plus one-of validation and emit real schema element names; generic list[Any] or placeholder node names silently drift from XSD and break both builder and parser.

Replace ValoresIBSCBS.g_ree_rep_res list[Any] with structured documentos models that mirror TCRTCListaDoc/TCRTCInfoReeRepRes, then emit and parse <documentos> wrappers instead of synthetic <item> nodes so builder, parser, and XSD all share one shape.
Keep temp-file mTLS approach for now, but wrap both httpx.Client construction and yield in one try/finally so PEM files are always unlinked even when client setup fails before the context yields.

After the gReeRepRes typed-model work, _CNPJ_PATTERN and _CPF_PATTERN became live again via ListaDocFornecIBSCBS. Remaining dead constants are now _FONE_PATTERN plus the unused tpNFSe value sets; cleanup should remove only the truly dead ones.

Remove only the still-dead tpNFSe constant sets and reuse shared regex constants from live Field(pattern=...) sites. _CNPJ_PATTERN/_CPF_PATTERN remain as real single-source-of-truth after the gReeRepRes typed-model addition.
Keep only the combined _OR_ path constants because the validators consume those directly; removing dead single-arm siblings cuts the wrong-edit surface without changing any business rules.
Delete the two thin factory wrappers and instantiate the frozen dataclasses directly at the lookup-table call sites. Keyword construction stays explicit, but the extra forwarding layer is gone.
Extract _parse_endereco(end) for the shared end/endNac response shape and reuse it for both dest and imovel branches. Helper stays local/private and returns the same dict shape as before.
Extract _emit_endereco(parent, endereco) inside XMLBuilder and reuse it for IBSCBS dest.end and imovel.end. Helper preserves the existing imperative emission order and exact XML shape.
Chose MkDocs Material + mkdocstrings with GitHub Pages artifact deploy so docs stay markdown-first, API pages render from docstrings, and site can publish from CI without hand-copied examples.

`uv sync --group docs` installs dependency groups but not the project extra `dev`; this repo needs `uv sync --extra dev --group docs` for local pytest validation because test deps live under project optional-dependencies.dev.

uv sync --group docs installs dependency groups but not the project extra dev; this repo needs uv sync --extra dev --group docs for local pytest validation because test deps live under project optional-dependencies.dev.
Pointed project Homepage at the docs site and kept Repository separate so PyPI/GitHub land on the rendered docs instead of the repo root while the README stays the package summary.
MkDocs Material section indexes fit this site well; they let each nav section keep a real overview page instead of a dead heading.
